instruction: make current file read-only in android studio

location.label: File menu in the macOS menu bar (second menu item after 'Android Studio')
location.name: File menu in the macOS menu bar (second menu item after 'Android Studio')
location.reasoning: To make the current file read-only in Android Studio, the user needs to access the File menu which contains the 'Make File Read-only' option. The macOS menu bar is at the top of the screen. The 'Android Studio' application menu is at x≈86-102, and 'Navigate' is at x≈328. With 'File', 'Edit', and 'View' menu items between them, 'File' should be located at approximately x=195, y=13. This will open the File menu where the read-only toggle option can be found.

history.reasoning: To make a file read-only in Android Studio, the user needs to access the File menu (File > Make File Read-Only) or right-click the editor tab. The current crop shows Android Studio with the Device Manager view dominant, but the editor tabs (app-debug, AndroidManifest.xml) are visible in the middle area. I need to crop to the top portion of Android Studio that includes the menu bar (File, Edit, View, etc.) and the editor tab area where AndroidManifest.xml is shown, so the next round can either access File > Make File Read-Only or right-click the tab to find the read-only option.

history.target_visible_element: File menu in Android Studio menu bar

To make the current file read-only, the user needs to access the File menu, which is located in the menu bar at the top of the Android Studio window, just below the title bar and above the tabs. This crop focuses on the left portion of the menu bar to show the File menu along with adjacent menus (Edit, View, Navigate, Code) for context and disambiguation.
